name: BR-271P APCR
description:
The 57x480mm family of fixed charge ammunition is issued to the Soviet 57mm anti-tank gun M1943 (ZiS-2, GRAU index 52-P-271), domestically produced as Type 1955 cannon. 
This example illustrates firing the BR-271P APCR projectile with the corresponding charge. Nominal velocity of >=1270 m/s. Nominal pressure at 3100 kgf/cm^2 in copper crusher gauge.
Computational value of 3300 kgf/cm^2 has been adopted, with a charge mass of 1.6 kg. 
Reference:
 《火炮内弹道计算手册》(1987)
 《火炸药手册 (增订本）第二分册》(1981).
 《炮弹火箭弹手册：第二分册 陆军炮弹与火箭弹》(1984)

name: BR-271M APBC-T
description:
The 57x480mm family of fixed charge ammunition is issued to the Soviet 57mm anti-tank gun M1943 (ZiS-2, GRAU index 52-P-271), domestically produced as Type 1955 cannon. 
This example illustrates firing the BR-271M APBC-T projectile with the corresponding charge. Nominal velocity of 1040 m/s. Nominal pressure of 3100 kgf/cm^2 in copper crusher gauge. Computational value of 3260 kgf/cm^2 has been adopted, with a charge mass of 1.47 kg 
Reference:
 《火炮内弹道计算手册》(1987)
 《火炸药手册 (增订本）第二分册》(1981).
 《炮弹火箭弹手册：第二分册 陆军炮弹与火箭弹》(1984)

name: WB009P HE-Frag
description:
The 57x480mm family of fixed charge ammunition is issued to the Soviet 57mm anti-tank gun M1943 (ZiS-2, GRAU index 52-P-271), domestically produced as Type 1955 cannon. 
This example illustrates firing the WB009 HE-Frag projectile with the corresponding charge. The different tabulated values for muzzle velocity realtes to the difference in construction of the driving band(s) -- single driving band projectiles develops 1700 kgf/cm^2 of pressure, and achieves a velocity of 700 m/s, while double driving band projectiles develops 1800 kgf/cm^2 of pressure. Computational value of 1930 - 2004 kgf/cm^2 are known. The value of 2000 kgf/cm^2 is adopted with a reduced charge of 0.98 kg

Reference:
 《火炮内弹道计算手册》(1987)
 《火炸药手册 (增订本）第二分册》(1981).
 《炮弹火箭弹手册：第二分册 陆军炮弹与火箭弹》(1984)
